Off Calendar Apr-09-2025 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Additional Physical Examination Of Plaintiff Shelley Marder
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RANDIE BENCANNAN'S Motion To Compel Additional Physical Examination Of Plaintiff Shelley Marder.

Defendant's motion to compel an additional physical examination of plaintiff is denied without prejudice.

A defendant is entitled to one physical examination of a plaintiff seeking personal injury damages. (CCP 2032.220(a).) To obtain a second examination, defendant must obtain leave of court based on "good cause." (CCP 2032.310(a); CCP 2032.320(a); Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 840-41.) In addition, a motion for a medical examination over objection "must" include a separate statement. (CRC 3.1345(a)(6).) Here, as defendant admits in the reply, defendant did not comply with the separate statement requirement.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
